flags.concern: Timeline auto-scroll coordination: wiring `createAutoScroller` only into pointer/drag handlers leaves marquee selection, snap/drop calculations, and drag indicators stale while RAF scrolling continues with a stationary pointer.
flags.evidence: `createAutoScroller` only mutates `scrollLeft`/`scrollTop` inside its RAF tick and does not invoke any drag-state recomputation (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/auto-scroll.ts:14-23, :25-52). `useMarqueeSelect` recomputes the marquee only inside `handlePointerMove` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useMarqueeSelect.ts:93-145). `useClipDrag` only calls `coordinator.update(...)` from `handlePointerMove` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:251-279). `useDragCoordinator` has no scroll subscription; it updates position only when `update(...)` is called (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator.ts:117-153). So Step 2's claim that calling `autoScroller.update(...)` before `updateSelection()` makes selection read the new scroll position is false against the current utility API.

flags.concern: Timeline external drop: Over-engineering: Step 4 proposes threading `editAreaRef` into `useExternalDrop`, but this hook already receives the timeline wrapper on every `dragover`, and existing repo code already resolves `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` from a wrapper.
flags.evidence: `TimelineEditor` attaches `onDragOver`/`onDragLeave`/`onDrop` to the wrapper div, so `useExternalDrop` already gets that wrapper as `event.currentTarget` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:233-238; /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:74-111). The drop-position code already uses wrapper -> `querySelector('.timeline-canvas-edit-area')` via `getTimelineDomNodes(...)` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts:50-65). Adding new hook/context plumbing is extra surface area the repo does not need for this feature.

flags.concern: Timeline auto-scroll: Scope creep: Step 5 adds a generalized `insets` API and changes left-edge semantics before the repository shows a real gutter bug.
flags.evidence: The current DOM intentionally keeps the label gutter inside the same scroll container: sticky track labels live in each row (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:149-152), the edit area itself is the overflow container (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:494-497), and the gutter is reinforced with a sticky `::before` pseudo-element (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/timeline-overrides.css:1-12). The repo already models content offset with `LABEL_WIDTH`/`TIMELINE_START_LEFT` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts:4-5). The user request was scrolling near any edge; introducing a generic inset system now expands scope and may change expected left-edge behavior without evidence.
